meaning: n.鍡ょ瑧,鍋风瑧,绐冪瑧,鍌荤瑧vi.鍚冨悆鍦扮瑧,鍋风瑧
examples: Mollie gave an uneasy little titter.
He made a sad business of it with his unsteady hand, and a smothered titter rippled over the house.
Mention sex therapy and most people will titter in embarrassment.
So your comparison of Mr Obama to Vladimir Putin, though worthy of a titter, doesn't hold up.
San Francisco was a titter (and Twitter) with tales of a facially rising ocean that would lift all boats.
